sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Level 2 has a different structure. I see:
- 3 red blocks at (17,20), (26,14), (35,14)
- Green blocks at (17,39), (26,48), (35,48) and others
- Multiple rows of colored blocks
Looking at the layout:
- Row 17: R at col 20, then S, b, p, Y, p at cols 23,26,29,32,35, then N at col 39
- Row 26: R at col 14, then w, b, b, w, S, p, g, S, g, g at cols 17,20,23,26,29,32,35,38,41,44, then N at col 48
- Row 35: R at col 14, then w, S, b, w, b, S, p, b, g, S at cols 17,20,23,26,29,32,35,38,41,44, then N at col 48
It seems like there are 3 independent puzzles (rows), each with a red block on the left, colored blocks in the middle, and a green block on the right. The mechanic from level 1 was to click the red block to rotate the colored blocks.
Let me try clicking each red block and see what happens. I'll start with the top red block at (17, 20).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Level 2 has 3 rows with red-green pairs. Let me try clicking the top red block first.
# The colored blocks between red and green in each row likely rotate when clicking red.

# Let me first identify the cycle for each row
# Row 17: R(20) -> S(23), b(26), p(29), Y(32), p(35) -> N(39)
# Row 26: R(14) -> w(17), b(20), b(23), w(26), S(29), p(32), g(35), S(38), g(41), g(44) -> N(48)
# Row 35: R(14) -> w(17), S(20), b(23), w(26), b(29), S(32), p(35), b(38), g(41), S(44) -> N(48)

# There are also vertical columns at col 23 and col 35
# Col 23: p(20), g(23), w(29), w(32), Y(38), g(41), S(44)
# Col 35: b(20), S(23), w(29), g(32), w(38), b(41), g(44)

# Let me try clicking the top red block at (17, 20)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 17, 'col': 20}])
print(f"Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Check what changed
if last_action_result.get('board_changed'):
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    print("\nBlocks after click:")
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['pixels'] < 100 and n['color'] not in ['c', 'G', 'B']:
            boundary = n['boundary']
            rows = [p[0] for p in boundary]
            cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
            center_r = sum(rows) // len(rows)
            center_c = sum(cols) // len(cols)
            print(f"  color={n['color']}, center=({center_r}, {center_c}), pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
